TimeEntry.Title: BST Servers pending for reboot after APD
TimeEntry.Notes: Log in to the Manage Engine to check the servers BST-HQ-VBR-01 which have not been rebooted as backups were running that time. So connected to the server and rebooted them and verified all the services are running fine. and VBR application is working fine.

TimeEntry.Title: Setup Veeam Agent for Microsoft on BST-HQ-FS-01 & BST-HQ-FS-02
TimeEntry.Notes: Logged into BST servers and then start working on them one by one as follow:BST-HQ-FS-01 & BST-HQ-FS-02.Work Performed:Installed and configured Veeam Agent on the client server.Verified connectivity between the client server and the cloud object storage endpoint.Configured S3-compatible object storage repository in Veeam.Enabled immutability settings on the S3 bucket to protect backup data from modification or deletion for the defined retention period.Created and configured a backup job targeting the S3-compatible immutable repository.Configured backup schedule and retention policy as per requirement.Performed initial backup run and verified successful completion.Validated that backup files are stored in the immutable S3 object storage and cannot be altered during the retention period.

TimeEntry.Title: Performed Weekly Firewall Backup for BST:- FortiGate 100F
TimeEntry.Notes: Logged into the firewall to perform the weekly backup for the BST:- FortiGate 100F. Downloaded the backup file and placed the same file on the Team Channel under the network config-firewall folder. Hence, closing this ticket.

TimeEntry.Title: BST-HQ-VONE,BST-HQ-RA-01,BST-HQ-RG-01: Servers were waiting for reboot after APD.
TimeEntry.Notes:
BST-HQ-VONE,BST-HQ-RA-01,BST-HQ-RG-01 :

  • Rebooted all the mentioned servers.

  • Logged into the servers after reboot and verified all services.

  • Manually restarted the services that did not start automatically.

  • Logged into the Veeam client and verified it successfully.
TimeEntry.Resource: S. Kumar Sharma CHD-TS1 Support - Remote

TimeEntry.Title: BG61,BG77 : Manually patch update as APD failed to do.
TimeEntry.Notes:
BG61:

  • Updated Ethernet Connection (7) I219-LM driver to version 12.19.2.66.

  • Deployed Cisco Secure Client (5.1.15.287) patch.

  • Updated CoffeeLake-S GT2 [UHD Graphics P630] driver to version 31.0.101.2137.

BG77:

  • Deployed Cisco Secure Client (5.1.15.287) patch.

  • Updated Raptor Lake-S GT1 [UHD Graphics 770] driver to version 32.0.101.7082.

All these were also updated over myrmm database.
TimeEntry.Resource: S. Kumar Sharma CHD-TS1 Support - Remote

TimeEntry.Title: Server pending for reboot after ADP
TimeEntry.Notes: Log in to the Manage Engine to check the servers BST-HQ-FS-01 which have not been rebooted. and installed failed patches. So connected to the servers and rebooted them and verified all the services are running fine.

TimeEntry.Title: Error in Veeam Cloud backup for BST
TimeEntry.Notes: While monitoring the Veeam backups for BST got an error for cloud backup job. SO connected to BST-HQ-VBR-01 server, rescan the backup repo. And started the backup. Now the backups are passed without any error.

TimeEntry.Title: Setup Veeam Agent for Microsoft on BST-HQ-FS-02
TimeEntry.Notes: Logged into BST server BST-HQ-FS-02. Work Performed: Installed and configured Veeam Agent on the client server. Verified connectivity between the client server and the cloud object storage endpoint. Configured S3-compatible object storage repository in Veeam. Enabled immutability settings on the S3 bucket to protect backup data from modification or deletion for the defined retention period. Created and configured a backup job targeting the S3-compatible immutable repository. Configured backup schedule and retention policy as per requirement. Performed initial backup run and verified successful completion. Validated that backup files are stored in the immutable S3 object storage and cannot be altered during the retention period.

TimeEntry.Title: Agent backups failed for both File server
TimeEntry.Notes: While monitoring the Veeam backups for BST, I noticed a file-level backup error. I connected to the BST-HQ-FS-01 and BST-HQ-FS-02 servers to investigate. After troubleshooting the issue, I reconfigured the affected backup job and then ran the backup again to confirm that it processed correctly.

TimeEntry.Title: Veeam Cloud backup for BST were stuck
TimeEntry.Notes: While monitoring the Veeam backups for BST got an error for cloud backup job. So i  connected to BST-HQ-VBR-01 server, rebooted it and then rescan the backup repo. And started the backup. Now the backups were running but stopped as per restrictions and during the working hours started.

TimeEntry.Title: Virtual Disk consolidation required - bst-hq-opsprb-0
TimeEntry.Notes: Logged in to vCenter and found the server(bst-hq-opsprb-0) is on Disk consolidation. Go the snapshots and start consolidation for the server and complete the process successfully Then logged in to the server and verify all the services are up and working fine

TimeEntry.Title: Case #08018766 1. Issue with Veeam Agent Backup Configuration Resetting Automatically. 2. File Syncing to Cloud with Real-Time Sync for Local Changes
TimeEntry.Notes: I have opened the ticket with Veeam support for Issue with BST-HQ-FS-02 server for Veeam agent resetting automatically issue.

TimeEntry.Title: Case #08018766 1. Issue with Veeam Agent Backup Configuration Resetting Automatically. 2. File Syncing to Cloud with Real-Time Sync for Local Changes
TimeEntry.Notes: Connected to the BST-HQ-FS-02 server to verify the backup status and found that the backup job had failed after running for approximately 12 hours. After some time, the Veeam Agent was no longer present as a running service on the server, although the application was still visible in the Programs and Features (appwiz.cpl). Reviewed the Event Viewer for any entries related to the Veeam Agent unexpectedly stopping or removing itself, but no relevant logs or errors were found indicating the cause of the agent reset or disappearance. Performed initial troubleshooting but the root cause could not be determined. A support case has been opened with the appropriate vendor for further investigation.

TimeEntry.Title: Kevin2 Persimmon has Missing patch while last run
TimeEntry.Notes:
I worked on the Missing patches and logged into the ManageEngine portal to review the approved patches for the machines. I downloaded and manually installed the applicable failed patches, then pending for reboot.  Some patches not applicable due to non compatibility. All previously missing patches were successfully updated.

Machines are  Kevin2

Patches are Adobe Acrobat Reader DC (25.001.21265)
Apple iTunes (X64) (12.13.10.3)
Mozilla Firefox (x64) (148.0)

TimeEntry.Title: Case #08018766 1. Issue with Veeam Agent Backup Configuration Resetting Automatically. 2. File Syncing to Cloud with Real-Time Sync for Local Changes
TimeEntry.Notes: Received the response from Veeam support, we have removed all other Veeam applications from the server and have set up the Veeam Agent again. We will monitor the system by the next day.

TimeEntry.Title: BST_HQ_VONE Probe will shutdown shortly.
TimeEntry.Notes: While logging into the OPS Manager admin console, found that the BST probe was down. Logged into the server to check whether the probe was installed, the SNMP service was running, and the community string was added. The probe service was up but still it was showing down in portal. So tried to restart the probe service and refreshed the probe from portal but nothing happened. Then reboot the server, after reboot verified the probe service is up. Then refreshed probe sensor from portal, it is up now and all devices are accessible.

TimeEntry.Title: Case #08018766 1. Issue with Veeam Agent Backup Configuration Resetting Automatically. 2. File Syncing to Cloud with Real-Time Sync for Local Changes
TimeEntry.Notes: Removed the old setup and agent as well and reinstalled and configured it again and having some issue to connectivity with S3 bucket and once resolved will add the backup job again.

TimeEntry.Title: Huntress Agent updated with Version V0.14.146 on BST-HQ-RG-01, BST-HQ-APP-01, BST-HQ-AD-02
TimeEntry.Notes:
Login into the Huntress portal, we verified that the Huntress Agent Version on the following machines on is old version V0.14.132. Connected to the machines
BST-HQ-RG-01, BST-HQ-APP-01, BST-HQ-AD-02 from manage engine, download and installed the latest version Ver0.14.146. After that verified in the portal it is updated to latest version.

TimeEntry.Title: Case #08018766 1. Issue with Veeam Agent Backup Configuration Resetting Automatically. 2. File Syncing to Cloud with Real-Time Sync for Local Changes
TimeEntry.Notes:
Got the veeam support mail and get were asking for the mounted drive registry logs...

so login to the server and export the reg file and shared it with the Veeam support....

TimeEntry.Title: Case #08018766 1. Issue with Veeam Agent Backup Configuration Resetting Automatically. 2. File Syncing to Cloud with Real-Time Sync for Local Changes
TimeEntry.Notes: As reinstalled and configured it again its still installed and haven't removed yet and having some issue to connectivity with S3 bucket and once resolved will add the backup job again.

TimeEntry.Title: Jeff-Home, BG75, BST-HQ-VONE : Old version of cisco found,not updated in APD
TimeEntry.Notes: Identified an older version of Cisco Umbrella on these machines. Downloaded the latest version, Cisco Secure Client (cisco-secure-client-win-5.1.15.287), and deployed it successfully. Verified that all machines are now syncing properly with the Cisco server.
TimeEntry.Resource: S. Kumar Sharma CHD-TS1 Support - Remote

TimeEntry.Title: Manage engine agent greyout issue for - BG72 and BST-HQ-LPT-02
TimeEntry.Notes: Login into Manage engine portal and found the agent on machines  BG72 and BST-HQ-LPT-02 not communication with portal. So connected to the machine, tried to restart the service, but nothing happened. So, Uninstall the Corrupt manage engine then download and the latest manage engine agent. After that agent on machine is communicating with the portal and working fine hence closing this ticket

TimeEntry.Title: BST Servers pending for reboot after APD
TimeEntry.Notes: Log in to the Manage Engine to check the servers BST-HQ-AD-01, BST-HQ-AD-02, BST-HQ-APP-01, BST-HQ-FS-01, BST-HQ-OPSPRB-0, BST-HQ-RG-01 which have not been rebooted. and installed failed patches. So connected to the servers and rebooted them and verified all the services are running fine.

TimeEntry.Title: Veeam backups job failed for BST
TimeEntry.Notes: Monitor the veeam backups for BST and found backups were failed with warning alerts due to low space in the backup storage. To resolve this, and cloud backups are also failed so rebooted the VBR server and it took so long once its back online. I cleaned up the old backups that were older than 14 days and then re-scanned the repository. After this backup space has increased.retried the backup job and all are passed successfully one job in progress and cloud backups are in progress.

TimeEntry.Title: Space issue in BST-HQ-RA-01
TimeEntry.Notes:
We are currently facing a space issue on the BST-HQ-RA-01 system. The C: drive was almost full, but after performing the necessary cleanup, 11GB of free space has been made available. To resolve this issue, the following steps were performed:

  1. TreeSize Download and Analysis:
    TreeSize was downloaded and installed to analyze the space usage on the system. It was used to perform a detailed scan of the C: drive to identify large files and directories consuming excessive disk space.

  2. Space Cleanup:
    After running the TreeSize scan, unnecessary files, patches, and temporary files were identified and deleted. This includes old software patches, cached files, logs, and other redundant files that were no longer required.

  3. Current Space Status:
    As a result of the cleanup, the C: drive now has 11GB of free space available. The system is in a more stable state with improved disk usage.

  4. Further Recommendations:
    It is recommended to implement regular disk cleanup procedures to avoid similar space issues in the future. This could involve automated scripts for removing temporary files or unused patches periodically.

TimeEntry.Title: Performed Weekly Firewall Backup for BST:- FortiGate 100F
TimeEntry.Notes: Logged into the firewall to perform the weekly backup for the BST:- FortiGate 100F. Downloaded the backup file and placed the same file on the Team Channel under the network config-firewall folder. Hence, closing this ticket.

TimeEntry.Title: BG72,DESKTOP-QPBGLP8 has Missing patch while last run
TimeEntry.Notes:
reviewed the missing patches from the ManageEngine portal for the machines BG72 and DESKTOP-QPBGLP8. The required updates were downloaded and installed to remediate the vulnerabilities.

The following updates have been successfully installed:

  • HD Graphics 630 [PCI\VEN_8086&DEV_5912] (31.0.101.2137)

  • Google Chrome (x64) (146.0.7680.80)

  • Microsoft Edge for Chromium Business (146.0.3856.59) (x64) – addressing CVE-2026-3910

  • Update for Microsoft WebView2 Runtime (x64) (146.0.3856.59)

  • Update for Microsoft 365 Apps for Enterprise Current Channel for x64 2602 of version (19725.20172)

After installation, both machines were scanned again through the ManageEngine portal to verify patch compliance and clear the reported vulnerabilities. The scan confirmed that the updates have been successfully applied.

TimeEntry.Title: Server BST-HQ-VONE,BT-HQ-RA-01,BST-HQ-FS-02 pending for reboot after APD
TimeEntry.Notes:
Login in manage engine portal and checked machines . Found below mentioned server pending for reboot after APD. So login into the servers, and restart the server.

After reboot login into the server, and checked required services are running. Then login again in manage engine portal and scan the machines for installed patches in APD. After scan the machines are now in healthy systems.

Machine : BST-HQ-VONE,BT-HQ-RA-01,BST-HQ-FS-02

TimeEntry.Title: Server BST-HQ-VBR, BT-HQ-APP-01,BST-HQ-FS-01, BST-HQ-OPSPRB-01 pending for reboot after APD
TimeEntry.Notes: Login in manage engine portal and checked machines . Found below mentioned server pending for reboot after APD. So login into the servers, and restart the server. After reboot login into the server, and checked required services are running. Then login again in manage engine portal and scan the machines for installed patches in APD. After scan the machines are now in healthy systems. Machine : BST-HQ-VBR, BT-HQ-APP-01,BST-HQ-FS-01, BST-HQ-OPSPRB-01

TimeEntry.Title: Installed latest version of Cisco umbrella version (5.1.15.287)BST-HQ-VBR-01
TimeEntry.Notes: Downloaded the latest Cisco Secure Client package (cisco-secure-client-win-5.1.15.287), uninstalled the older version from the machines (BST-HQ-VBR-01) and successfully installed the updated version. I also verified that the machines are properly synchronized with the server.

TimeEntry.Title: BST – Network Detective Report Generated and Updated Under Teams
TimeEntry.Notes: As per assigned task by lead, ND reports was generated successfully for BST Client. Checked and verified the reports are proper. Then uploaded the report to the appropriate client Teams channel for BST. It is now ready for review and take actions on alerts.

TimeEntry.Title: Missing and failed patch update on the systems:- BST-HQ-RA-01
TimeEntry.Notes:
Login to the manage engine portal and check approved patches for the below mentioned machine then download and installed. connected to the machine through MY RMM and installed the failed patches manually which are applicable to the computer. Then reboot the machine and scan the machine for installed patches. After scan Check and verify the machine with the MY RMM portal. The failed patches are updated successfully.

Machine is: - BST-HQ-RA-01

Patch installed:- 

 43630 Update for Microsoft 365 Apps for Enterprise Current Channel for x64 2602 of version(19725.20172)

After scan Check and verify the machine with the MY RMM portal. The failed patches are updated successfully.

Machine is: - BST-HQ-VONE

Patch installed:- 

356587 Cisco Secure Client Diagnostics and Reporting Tool (5.1.15.287) (Manual Upload Required)

TimeEntry.Title: Upgrade VMWare tools - BST-HQ-APP-01, BST-HQ-FS-02
TimeEntry.Notes: Login to the vCenter and check the following machines (BST-HQ-APP-01, BST-HQ-FS-02) needs to upgrade the VMware tool on it. Go to the VM and the initiate upgrade VMware tool on the machine successfully.

Connected to the machines (BST-HQ-VONE, BST-HQ-FS-02, BST-HQ-OPSPRB-01, BST-HQ-AD-01) from manage engine, download and installed the latest version V0.14.146. TimeEntry.Title: Crowdstrike latest version (7.34.20610.0) updated on machine: BST-HQ-AD-01, BST-HQ-AD-02, BST-HQ-APP-01, BST-HQ-FS-01
TimeEntry.Notes: Login into Crowd strike portal to check the machines with outdated version. Found machines BST-HQ-AD-01, BST-HQ-AD-02, BST-HQ-APP-01, BST-HQ-FS-01 running with older version of crowd strike. So connected to machines and uninstalled the old version and downloaded and installed the latest sensor version 7.34.20610.0 of CrowdStrike, verified from the portal. it is updated now. Hence closing this ticket.

Login into Crowd strike portal to check the machines with outdated version. Found machines BG75,BG72,BG77,BST-HQ-FS-02
running with older version of crowd strike. So connected to machines from manage engine and uninstalled the old version  and downloaded and installed the latest sensor version 7.34.20610.0
of CrowdStrike, verified from the portal. it is updated now. Hence closing this ticket.

TimeEntry.Title: My Remote version 26.1.20.9571 updated on BST Servers
TimeEntry.Notes: Review the my remote portal, and found the machine BST-HQ-AD-01, BST-HQ-AD-02, BST-HQ-FS-01, BST-HQ-OPSPRB-0, BST-HQ-RA-01, BST-HQ-RG-01, BST-HQ-VBR-01, BST-HQ-VONE running with old version of My Remote. Tried to reinstall the agent from my remote portal, but not succeeded. Tried to restart the service and reinstall again, but unable to update it. So connected to the machine from manage engine . Uninstalled the old version and reinstalled the latest version 26.1.20.9571 After that refresh the machine from the portal. It was updated to latest version of My Remote. Hence closing this ticket.

TimeEntry.Title: My Remote version 26.1.20.9571 updated on machine BG72,BG75,BG77,KEVIN2
TimeEntry.Notes:
Review the my remote portal, and found  the machine BG72,BG75,BG77,KEVIN2
running with old version of My Remote. Tried to reinstall the agent from my remote portal, but not succeeded. Tried to restart the service and reinstall again, but unable to update it. So connected to the machine from manage engine  .
Uninstalled the old version and reinstalled the latest version 26.1.20.9571. After that refresh the machine from the portal. It was updated to latest
version of My Remote. Hence closing this ticket.

TimeEntry.Title: BST_HQ_VONE Probe will shutdown shortly.
TimeEntry.Notes: While logging into the OPS Manager admin console, found that the BST probe was down. Logged into the server to check whether the probe was installed, the SNMP service was running, and the community string was added. The probe service was up but still it was showing down in portal. So tried to restart the probe service and refreshed the probe from portal but nothing happened. Then reboot the server, after reboot verified the probe service is up. Then refreshed probe sensor from portal, it is up now and all devices are accessible .

TimeEntry.Title: Backup location [\\10.1.1.195\BK_BST] is getting low on free disk space (16.4 GB free of 3.5 TB).
TimeEntry.Notes: Monitor the veeam backups for BST and found backups were failed with warning alerts due to low space in the backup storage. To resolve this, and cloud backups are also failed so rebooted the VBR server and it took so long once its back online. I cleaned up the old backups that were older than 14 days and then re-scanned the repository. After this backup space has increased.retried the backup job and all are passed successfully and cloud backups are in progress.

TimeEntry.Title: Device Non-Compliant Due to Missing Patches BST-HQ-OPSPRB-01 & BST-HQ-RG-01
TimeEntry.Notes:
Devices BST-HQ-OPSPRB-01 and BST-HQ-RG-01 were identified with missing patches.

Accessed the ManageEngine portal and verified pending updates on both systems. Proceeded with patch installation for the following:

  • Google Chrome (x64) – Versions 146.0.7680.153 / 146.0.7680.154

Performed a manual patch scan post-installation to ensure successful deployment and to clear the pending patch status.

Verified that the updates have been successfully applied and both systems are now compliant with no remaining vulnerabilities related to the reported patch.

TimeEntry.Title: Performed Weekly Firewall Backup for BST:- FortiGate 100F
TimeEntry.Notes: Logged into the firewall to perform the weekly backup for the BST:- FortiGate 100F. Downloaded the backup file and placed the same file on the Team Channel under the network config-firewall folder. Hence, closing this ticket.

TimeEntry.Title: Gagan on a call with BST and requires assistance, as his CC application is not functioning properly.
TimeEntry.Notes: Issue resolved now working fine.

TimeEntry.Title: Huntress Agent updated with Version V0.14.150 on BG75,BG72,Kevin2,BST-HQ-VONE
TimeEntry.Notes:
Login into the Huntress portal, we verified that the Huntress Agent Version on the following machines on  is old version V0.14.132. Connected to the machines 
BG75,BG72,Kevin2,BST-HQ-VONE from manage engine download and installed the latest version Ver0.14.150. After that verified in the portal it is updated to latest version.

TimeEntry.Title: Prepare credential sheet from passportal requested by client
TimeEntry.Notes: Sheed is completed with all credential details as asked for and shared over the channel.
TimeEntry.Resource: S. Kumar Sharma CHD-TS1 Support - Remote

TimeEntry.Title: off boarding
TimeEntry.Notes: gathered documents for off boarding.

TimeEntry.Title: Failed Veeam backups for BST
TimeEntry.Notes: While monitoring the backups for BST, cloud backups got failed. SO connected to the server and troubleshoot the issue. The issue with the expired certificate for the cloud backup job was resolved by updating the SSL certificate on the Veeam Backup and Replication server. The new certificate was imported into the appropriate certificate store on the server, ensuring it was correctly associated with the myveeamspc.technijian.com service provider. After restarting the necessary Veeam services, the repository was rescanned, and the backup job for the cloud repository successfully running without any further certificate errors.

TimeEntry.Title: Virtual Disk consolidation required - BST-HQ-AD-02
TimeEntry.Notes: Logged in to vCenter and found the server BST-HQ-AD-02 is in Disk consolidation state. checked the snapshots and started consolidation for the server and complete the process successfully. Then logged into the server and verified all the services are up and working fine.

TimeEntry.Title: Gagan with BST needs assistance with cietrade not connecting
TimeEntry.Notes:
Gagan with BST needs assistance with cietrade not connecting....

So connect to the system and asked gagan to try to connect the Quickbook and Cietrade again...

gagan checked and confirmed its working fine now he is able to connect....hence closing the ticket....

TimeEntry.Title: BG77, BG72 : Manually patch update as APD couldn't do it.
TimeEntry.Notes:
BST-BG77

  • Cisco Secure Client – 5.1.16.194
  • 2026-03 Cumulative Update for Windows 11 24H2 (KB5085516 – Build 26100.8039)
  • Microsoft Edge for Chromium Business (x64) – 146.0.3856.78
  • Update for Microsoft WebView2 Runtime (x64) – 146.0.3856.78
  • All updates installed successfully; reboot required to reflect in myRMM.

BG72

  • Cisco Secure Client – 5.1.16.194
  • 2026-03 Cumulative Update for Windows 11 25H2 (KB5085516 – Build 26200.8039)
  • Google Chrome – 146.0.7680.164 → 146.0.7680.165
  • All updates installed successfully; reboot required to reflect in myRMM.
TimeEntry.Resource: S. Kumar Sharma CHD-TS1 Support - Remote

TimeEntry.Title: Virtual Disk consolidation required - BST-HQ-APP-01, BST-HQ-FS-01, BST-HQ-FS-02.
TimeEntry.Notes:
Logged in to vCenter and found the server ORX-DC-3CX is on Disk consolidation. Go the snapshots and start consolidation for the server and complete the process successfully and then login to the server and verify all the services are up and working fine.

BST-HQ-APP-01, BST-HQ-FS-01, BST-HQ-FS-02.

TimeEntry.Title: SERVER Alert - Trouble - BST-HQ-VONE: (10.1.1.29) - Disk Utilization is 72%, threshold value for this monitor is 60%
TimeEntry.Notes: As per the alert connected to BST-HQ-VONE, download and install TreeSize, a disk space management tool that provides a detailed overview of the disk usage across folders and files on the system. After installation, TreeSize was run to analyse the disk. TreeSize scans the system and generates a report showing the size of all folders and files in a hierarchical structure so carefully review all folders and files occupying excessive space. Based on the review, we proceeded to clear unnecessary files that were identified as no longer needed. This included: Temporary Files: Files in the system's temporary folder are often created by applications during installation, updates, or other processes; clear these temporary files to free up space. Recycle Bin: emptied the Recycle Bin, where deleted files are temporarily stored. This ensured that deleted files were permanently removed from the disk and no longer took up space. Downloaded Items: System error memory dump files, Old Windows updates, previous Windows installations other system cache files. 58% of the space is free. Now Sufficient Free Space Available.

TimeEntry.Title: RE: Service Cancellation -BST
TimeEntry.Notes:
Collected workstation details from the remote portal for the same system. Verified and gathered current OS installation details from the CrowdStrike portal and updated the information in the shared Excel sheet provided by Rishad.

Additionally, retrieved Access Point (AP) details by logging into the firewall, navigating to AP devices, and collecting the AP model and serial number. All relevant information has been documented in the shared Excel sheet. and in this sheet switch, firewall and server detail already mention.

TimeEntry.Title: MyRemote Updated to Latest Version on BST Machines
TimeEntry.Notes:
As part of the update task, the MyRemote client was reviewed and updated on the following machines:

  1. Machines Updated:
    • DESKTOP-QPBGLP8
    • JKLEIN-LENOVO
    • KEVIN2
  2. Update Performed:
    • Successfully updated the MyRemote client to the latest version 26.1.22.9573 on all the above machines.
  3. Cleanup:
    • Previous machine entries have been removed, and the updated machines have been added as per requirement.
  4. Verification:
    • Verified that the updated version is installed and systems are functioning properly.
    • Remote connectivity is working as expected.

Current Status:
The MyRemote client has been successfully updated to the latest version on all listed machines, and everything is working fine.

TimeEntry.Title: off boarding
TimeEntry.Notes: send the details to Renwick, tech heights.

TimeEntry.Title: BG61,BST-HQ-RG-01 : Myremote update with latest version
TimeEntry.Notes: I accessed the machines and updated ScreenConnect to the latest version (26.1.22.9573).
TimeEntry.Resource: S. Kumar Sharma CHD-TS1 Support - Remote

TimeEntry.Title: Screenconnect Latest version (v 26.1.22.9573) update: - BST-HQ-APP-01, DESKTOP-QPBGLP8, BST-HQ-AD-01, BST-HQ-AD-02
TimeEntry.Notes: Checked and found that the devices (BST-HQ-APP-01, DESKTOP-QPBGLP8, BST-HQ-AD-01, BST-HQ-AD-02) were running an outdated version of Screen Connect. I initiated the update for these devices from the portal, but unable to update, I noticed some issues when verifying the version on the portal. connected to the machines through My Rmm and uninstall the screen connect software. after that download and install the fresh My Remote setup and install on it and verify that agents have been updated successfully with the latest version (v 26.1.22.9573)Hence closing the ticket

TimeEntry.Title: Huntress Agent updated with Version 0.14.150 on BST-HQ-AD-01, BST-HQ-APP-01, BST-HQ-FS-01, BST-HQ-OPSPRB-01
TimeEntry.Notes: Login into the Huntress portal, we verified that the Huntress Agent Version on the following machines on is old version 0.14.146. Connected to the machines BST-HQ-AD-01, BST-HQ-APP-01, BST-HQ-FS-01, BST-HQ-OPSPRB-01 from manage engine, download and installed the latest Version 0.14.150. After that verified in the portal it is updated to latest version.

TimeEntry.Title: Maintain Policy: reset the credentials
TimeEntry.Notes:
1. Logged into office365. Changed the credential for the global administrator for the BST Client 

2. Logged into the active directory and changed the administrator credentials.

3. Logged into the Firewall and changed the admin credentials.

TimeEntry.Title: Performed Weekly Firewall Backup for BST:- FortiGate 100F
TimeEntry.Notes: Logged into the firewall to perform the weekly backup for the BST:- FortiGate 100F. Downloaded the backup file and placed the same file on the Team Channel under the network config-firewall folder. Hence, closing this ticket.

TimeEntry.Title: Cisco Secure Client Manual Update Required on BST-HQ-FS-01, BST-HQ-VBR-01 & BST-HQ-APP-01
TimeEntry.Notes:
After reviewing the management portal, it has been identified that the following systems require manual updates:

BST-HQ-FS-01, BST-HQ-VBR-01 & BST-HQ-APP-01

The following components need to be manually uploaded and installed:

  • Cisco Secure Client – AnyConnect VPN (5.1.16.194) – Manual installation required

Remote access will be used to download and install the required setup files on the affected systems. Once the installation is completed, a scan will be initiated through the management portal to verify that the systems reflect the latest version.

TimeEntry.Title: Upgrade VMWare tools - BST-HQ-AD-02, BST-HQ-APP-01, BST-HQ-FS-01, BST-HQ-FS-02.
TimeEntry.Notes:
Login to the vCenter and check the following machines needs to upgrade the VMware tool on it. Go to the VM and the initiate upgrade VMware tool on the machine successfully.

BST-HQ-AD-02, BST-HQ-APP-01, BST-HQ-FS-01, BST-HQ-FS-02.

TimeEntry.Title: My Remote version 26.1.22.9573 updated on machine BST-HQ-FS-01,BST-HQ-FS-02,BST-HQ-VONE,BST-HQ-RA-01
TimeEntry.Notes:
Review the my remote portal, and found  the machine BST-HQ-FS-01,BST-HQ-FS-02,BST-HQ-VONE,BST-HQ-RA-01
running with old version of My Remote. Tried to reinstall the agent from my remote portal, but not succeeded. Tried to restart the service and reinstall again, but unable to update it. So connected to the machine from manage engine  .
Uninstalled the old version and reinstalled the latest version 26.1.22.9573. After that refresh the machine from the portal. It was updated to latest
version of My Remote. Hence closing this ticket.
